title: NEET UG EXAM 2026 CANCELLED: WHO IS RESPONSIBLE?
description:
NEET UG 2026 paper leak has raised serious questions about India’s exam system. The NEET-UG exam was held on May 3, 2026, but after paper leak allegations, NTA cancelled the exam on May 12, 2026, affecting more than 22 lakh medical aspirants.

Reports claim that the main accused, Shubham Khairnar, a third-year BAMS student from Nashik, allegedly bought the NEET paper for ₹10 lakh and sold it through courier to a person in Gurugram for ₹15 lakh. Later, the questions were reportedly mixed into a 410-question handwritten guess paper, which included around 120 actual NEET questions.

This guess paper allegedly circulated on May 2 night through coaching centres, PG hostels, and a WhatsApp group called “Private Mafia.” Now, students are protesting, and the United Doctors Front has demanded the dissolution of NTA and NMC, an independent investigation, strict action against guilty officials, and a UPSC-level secure exam system.

In this video, we explain the full NEET 2026 paper leak timeline, how the paper was leaked, where it was circulated, student protests, doctors’ demands, CBI investigation, and why this may be a major system failure.
